tt_insn_vec.help: Portion of vector registers that is actually used. Available only for instructions belonging to a SIMD/vector instructions set (like x86 AVX)

tt_Recip_throughput.help: Average number of cycles to retire that instruction if repeated (assuming independent instructions, with no read-after-write dependency)

hint.workaround: Try to remove indirect accesses. If applicable, precompute elements out of the innermost loop.
hint.details:
• Irregular (variable stride) or indirect: 1 occurrence(s)
Non-unit stride (uncontiguous) accesses are not efficiently using data caches
hint.title: Slow data structures access
hint.txt: Detected data structures (typically arrays) that cannot be efficiently read/written

hint.title: Type of elements and instruction set
hint.txt: 2 SSE or AVX instructions are processing arithmetic or math operations on double precision FP elements in scalar mode (one at a time).

hint.title: Matching between your loop (in the source code) and the binary loop
hint.txt:
The binary loop is composed of 2 FP arithmetical operations:
  • 1: addition or subtraction
  • 1: multiply
The binary loop is loading 20 bytes (2 double precision FP elements).

hint.title: Arithmetic intensity
hint.txt: Arithmetic intensity is 0.10 FP operations per loaded or stored byte.

expert.txt:

nb uops6
loop length30
used x86 registers6
used mmx registers0
used xmm registers2
used ymm registers0
used zmm registers0
nb stack references0
ADD-SUB / MUL ratio1.00

expert.help: Front-end corresponds to instructions fetching and decoding into macro/micro operations
expert.title: Front-end
expert.txt:
ASSUMED MACRO FUSION FIT IN UOP CACHE
micro-operation queue1.50 cycles
front end1.50 cycles

expert.help: Back-end corresponds to macro/micro operations execution (includes allocating registers, fetching operands etc.)
expert.title: Back-end
expert.txt:
P0P1P2P3P4P5P6P7
uops1.001.001.501.500.001.001.000.00
cycles1.001.001.501.500.001.001.000.00
Execution ports to units layout:
  • P0 (256 bits): VPU, BRU, ALU, DIV/SQRT
  • P1 (256 bits): ALU, VPU
  • P2 (256 bits): store address, load
  • P3 (256 bits): store address, load
  • P4 (256 bits): store data
  • P5: ALU
  • P6: ALU, BRU
  • P7: store address
Cycles executing div or sqrt instructionsNA
Longest recurrence chain latency (RecMII)4.00

expert.help: Proportion of vectorizable instructions that are actually vectorized. Higher is better.

all0%
load0%
storeNA (no store vectorizable/vectorized instructions)
mul0%
add-sub0%
fmaNA (no fma vectorizable/vectorized instructions)
div/sqrtNA (no div/sqrt vectorizable/vectorized instructions)
otherNA (no other vectorizable/vectorized instructions)

expert.help: Compare the CQA-computed load/store/compute metrics with the capacity of the core. For instance if CQA computes 24 bytes in the loop in 2 cycles (that is 12 cycles per cycle) and if the core can process up to 16 bytes per cycle, that metric is 75%.
expert.title: Cycles and memory resources usage
expert.txt:
Assuming all data fit into the L1 cache, each iteration of the binary loop takes 4.00 cycles. At this rate:
  • 7% of peak load performance is reached (5.00 out of 64.00 bytes loaded per cycle (GB/s @ 1GHz))

expert.help: List of bottlenecks detected by CQA. A bottleneck is an issue which reduces front-end throughput below the core capacity.
expert.title: Front-end bottlenecks
ASM code
expert.txt:
In the binary file, the address of the loop is: 3350
Instruction<>P0P1P2P3P4P5P6P7Latency<><>
MOVSXD (%RBP,%RAX,4),%R91000.500.5000004-50.50N/A
MOVSD (%R15,%R9,8),%XMM01000.500.5000004-50.50scal (25.0%)
MULSD (%R12,%RAX,8),%XMM010.500.500.500.50000040.50scal (25.0%)
ADD $0x1,%RAX10.250.250000.250.25010.25N/A
ADDSD %XMM0,%XMM110.500.5000000040.50scal (25.0%)
CMP %EAX,%R10D10.250.250000.250.25010.25N/A
JG 3350 <_ZL9conj_gradPKiS0_PKdPdS2_S3_S3_S3_S3_+0x370>10.50000000.50000.50-1N/A

header: 3% of peak computational performance is used (0.50 out of 16.00 FLOP per cycle (GFLOPS @ 1GHz))

gain.workaround:
• Try another compiler or update/tune your current one
  • Remove inter-iterations dependences from your loop and make it unit-stride:
    • If your arrays have 2 or more dimensions, check whether elements are accessed contiguously and, otherwise, try to permute loops accordingly
    • If your loop streams arrays of structures (AoS), try to use structures of arrays instead (SoA)
gain.details:
All SSE/AVX instructions are used in scalar version (process only one data element in vector registers).
Since your execution units are vector units, only a vectorized loop can use their full power.
gain.title: Vectorization
gain.txt:
Your loop is not vectorized.
4 data elements could be processed at once in vector registers.
<<image_1x64_256>>By vectorizing your loop, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 4.00 to 1.00 cycles (4.00x speedup).

gain.title: Execution units bottlenecks
gain.txt: Found no such bottlenecks but see expert reports for more complex bottlenecks.

potential.workaround:
• Pass to your compiler a micro-architecture specialization option:
    • Please look into your compiler manual for march=native or equivalent
  • Try to change order in which elements are evaluated (using parentheses) in arithmetic expressions containing both ADD/SUB and MUL operations to enable your compiler to generate FMA instructions wherever possible. For instance a + b*c is a valid FMA (MUL then ADD). However (a+b)* c cannot be translated into an FMA (ADD then MUL).
potential.title: FMA
potential.txt: Presence of both ADD/SUB and MUL operations.
